CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, value INTEGER);
SELECT DISTINCT id, nth_value(value, 2) OVER (ORDER BY id) AS second_non_null FROM test_data;
CREATE TABLE employee (emp_id INTEGER, department_id INTEGER, salary INTEGER);

SELECT department_id, nth_value(salary, 1 IGNORE NULLS) OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY emp_id) AS first_salary, nth_value(salary, 2 IGNORE NULLS) OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY emp_id) AS second_salary FROM employee;
SELECT emp_id, nth_value(salary, 1) OVER (ORDER BY emp_id ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS nth_salary FROM employee;
CREATE TABLE sales (sale_id INTEGER, amount FLOAT, region VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1, 150.00, 'North'), (2, NULL, 'South'), (3, 200.50, 'North'), (4, NULL, 'West'), (5, 50.75, 'East');
SELECT region, nth_value(amount, 2 IGNORE NULLS) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY sale_id) AS second_valid_sale FROM sales;
WITH sales_data AS (SELECT sale_id, amount FROM sales WHERE amount IS NOT NULL) SELECT nth_value(amount, 1) OVER (ORDER BY sale_id) AS first_sale FROM sales_data;
SELECT nth_value(value, 3 IGNORE NULLS) OVER (ORDER BY id) AS third_non_null_value FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_data ORDER BY id LIMIT 4);
CREATE TABLE orders (order_id INTEGER, customer_id INTEGER, order_date DATE);
SELECT customer_id, nth_value(order_date, 1 IGNORE NULLS) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_id) AS first_order_date FROM orders;
